_comment: Bit-flag registry for the `cards.flags`, `actions.flags`, and `magnetic_actions.flags` u8 columns. Each entry pins an explicit bit position in [0, 7]: single-bit flags use `bit` (a number), multi-bit fields use `bits` (an array of bit positions, low-to-high). Append-only — once a bit (or set of bits) is assigned a name, never reuse it for a different meaning. Removed flags / fields should leave a `_reserved_<bit>` tombstone so a future feature can't silently inherit the old semantics.

actions._comment: Bits in `Action.flags` (u8). Freshly-started actions have all bits clear. Server-side: `actions::start_action` zero-initializes the field.

magnetic_inputs.description: Action's input slots attract compatible free cards toward the actor on the board.

canceled.description: Action ended for a reason other than normal recipe completion — matcher upgrade picked a different recipe, claim drift refused completion, a reagent got stolen by another action, etc. Always set in the same UPDATE as `dead` (bit 7), never on its own. Mutually exclusive with `complete` (bit 2). Lets the client branch its end animation between 'completed' and 'canceled'. Set by the chokepoint helper `actions::mark_action_dead` when its `canceled` argument is true.

complete.description: Action ended via normal recipe completion — outputs generated and reagents consumed. Always set in the same UPDATE as `dead` (bit 7), never on its own. Mutually exclusive with `canceled` (bit 1). Set by `actions::mark_action_dead` when `canceled` is false.

magnetic_subid.description: 3-bit field (low-to-high: bit 4 = LSB, bit 6 = MSB) encoding which of the magnetic outer's inner recipes queued this action. Set together with `magnetic_inputs` (bit 0) at queue time by `magnetic::queue_inner_action`; read at completion time by `complete_action` to dispatch to the correct inner's output_success / reagents / recipe_type. Range 0..=7 — caps the magnetic outer at 8 inner recipes (`MAGNETIC_MAX_INNERS` in `definitions.rs`). Pack/unpack helpers are `magnetic::pack_action_flags` / `magnetic::unpack_action_subid`.

dead.description: Server has marked this action as ended (recipe completed, canceled, or upgraded). Set as an UPDATE — rather than the row being deleted directly — so the write carries `valid_at` (server's unix-seconds 'this happened at' timestamp) and the client can time its end animation against server-truth instead of receive time. The matching `action_scheduler` and `card_holds` rows are deleted eagerly so no further completions fire and the matcher stops treating the actor as held; only the public `Action` row lingers, reaped by `reap_dead_action` after `actions::ACTION_REAP_DELAY_SECS`. Mirrors `cards.dead` at bit 7 so client-side flag handling can use one mask across both tables.

magnetic_actions._comment: Bits in `MagneticAction.flags` (u8). Freshly-installed rows have all bits clear. Server-side: `magnetic::install` zero-initializes the field.

canceled.description: Magnetic phase ended for a reason other than queueing an inner action or hitting the loop cap — typically an explicit `magnetic::cancel` (anchor gone, recipe vanished from registry, etc.). Always set in the same UPDATE as `dead` (bit 7), never on its own. Mutually exclusive with `complete` (bit 2). Lets the client branch its end animation between 'completed' and 'canceled'. Set by `magnetic::mark_magnetic_action_dead` when its `canceled` argument is true.

complete.description: Magnetic phase ended naturally — either an inner action was queued (output_success fired) or the loop cap was reached (output_failure fired). Always set in the same UPDATE as `dead` (bit 7), never on its own. Mutually exclusive with `canceled` (bit 1). Set by `magnetic::mark_magnetic_action_dead` when `canceled` is false.

dead.description: Server has marked this magnetic_action as ended (queued an inner action, timed out, or canceled). Set as an UPDATE — rather than the row being deleted directly — so the write carries `valid_at` (server's unix-seconds 'this happened at' timestamp) and the client can time its end animation against server-truth instead of receive time. The matching `magnetic_action_scheduler` row is deleted eagerly so no further ticks fire; only the public `MagneticAction` row lingers, reaped by `reap_dead_magnetic_action` after `magnetic::MAGNETIC_ACTION_REAP_DELAY_SECS`. Mirrors `cards.dead` and `actions.dead` at bit 7 so client-side flag handling can use one mask everywhere.
